title: Sweet Dense Saudi Arabian Mushroom Pilaf
description: A kid-friendly, FODMAP-friendly Saudi Arabian dish featuring sweet and dense mushroom pilaf, perfect for a weeknight dinner. This recipe uses a deglaze technique to enhance the flavors.
instructions: Heat ghee or oil in a large saucepan over medium heat.
Add chopped onion and cook until translucent, about 3-4 minutes.
Add sliced mushrooms and cook until they release their moisture and start browning, about 5 minutes.
Add date syrup and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly, to deglaze the pan.
Add basmati rice, salt, black pepper, and cardamom powder. Stir to combine.
Add water to the saucepan and bring to a boil.
Reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 15-20 minutes or until the rice is cooked and dense.
Turn off the heat and let it rest, covered, for 5 minutes.
Fluff the pilaf with a fork and serve hot.
